report_context.title: Birch skill seed comparison
report_context.question: Should Candidate B be promoted as the next GEPA seed?

scoring_formula.hygiene_score: average artifact compliance score after unsupported classes, external assets, and missing CSS penalties

caveats: Provider token schemas differ; cached, reasoning, and effective tokens are not apples-to-apples.
Checker warnings are mechanical contract signals, not a complete human quality score.
Same:same screenshot pairs primarily validate geometry and rendering contracts, not aesthetic quality.
Wall time includes model latency and harness overhead, so small differences should be treated as noisy.

runs.label: Seed A
decision_notes: All four artifacts generated and passed failure checks.
Mobile warnings remain around split rail alignment and dense chart text.
Good baseline, but warning gradient leaves room for optimization.

runs.label: Candidate B
decision_notes: Checker warnings dropped from five to two with no generation failures.
Cost rose by 11.5% and wall time rose by 18.7%.
One code-review artifact still needs inspection for long diff wrapping.

per_eval.regression_note: Candidate chart is clearer but uses more SVG text.
No mechanical regression; inspect finding severity wording.
Runtime path improved; mobile flow caption remains dense.
Plan quality improved; no checker regression.

recommendation.rationale: Candidate B improves final score by 0.048 and reduces checker warnings from five to two without adding failures. The latency and cost increases are material but acceptable for GEPA seed quality.
recommendation.next_experiment: Promote Candidate B for a single-model GEPA run, then rerun on Kimi and Gemini before broad model-suite validation. Inspect code-review diff wrapping manually before committing.
